name: BrowserDownload
summary: The BrowserDownload collector shows the BrowserDownload entries of a Windows operating system.

fields.name: browser
fields.description: Name of the browser used for downloading. The possible values are: Google Chrome Mozilla Firefox Microsoft Edge
enum: Google Chrome Mozilla Firefox Microsoft Edge

fields.name: browser_profile
fields.description: The browser profile used for downloading.

fields.name: end_time
fields.description: Time when the download ended. The string represents the FILETIME structure which is 64-bit. This value represents the number of 100-nanosecond intervals since January 1, 1601 (UTC).

fields.name: file_path
fields.description: The name of the downloaded file with the full path.

fields.name: mime_type
fields.description: This string indicates Multipurpose internet Mail Extensions type. It's a way of identifying files according to their nature and format.

fields.name: referrer
fields.description: The webpage that requested the download.

fields.name: site_url
fields.description: The URL of the site from where the file is downloaded.

fields.name: start_time
fields.description: Time when the download started. The string represents the FILETIME structure which is 64-bit. This value represents the number of 100-nanosecond intervals since January 1, 1601 (UTC).

fields.name: state
fields.description: The state indicates whether the download was completed successfully.

fields.name: total_bytes
fields.description: Total size of the file for which the download was initiated .

fields.name: user_profile
fields.description: The user profile used for downloading.

examples.title: Example: Show browser downloads from URLs that have the trellix.com domain
Which browsers are used in my environment
Prevalence - downloads by binary type
examples.v2_query:
BrowserDownload mime_type, file_path
    WHERE BrowserDownload mime_type contains "octet-stream"

examples.title: Prevalence - downloads by zip archive type
examples.v2_query:
BrowserDownload mime_type, file_path
    WHERE BrowserDownload mime_type equals "application/zip"

examples.title: Prevalence - downloads by many compressed archive type
examples.v2_query:
BrowserDownload mime_type, file_path
    WHERE BrowserDownload mime_type equals "application/zip"
        OR BrowserDownload mime_type equals "application/x-zip-compressed"
        OR BrowserDownload mime_type equals "application/x-tar"

examples.title: Prevalence - websites offering downloads of binary type
examples.v2_query:
BrowserDownload site_url, mime_type
    WHERE BrowserDownload mime_type contains "octet-stream"

examples.title: Specify single target website
examples.v2_query:
BrowserDownload site_url, mime_type
    WHERE BrowserDownload mime_type contains "octet-stream"
        AND BrowserDownload site_url contains "github.com"

examples.title: Specify many target websites
examples.v2_query:
BrowserDownload site_url, mime_type
    WHERE BrowserDownload mime_type contains "octet-stream"
        AND BrowserDownload site_url contains "github.com"
            OR BrowserDownload site_url contains "drive.google.com"
            OR BrowserDownload site_url contains "super.com"

examples.title: Specify single target filename
examples.v2_query:
BrowserDownload site_url, mime_type, file_path
    WHERE BrowserDownload mime_type contains "octet-stream"
        AND BrowserDownload file_path contains "zoom.exe"

examples.title: Specify many target filenames
examples.v2_query:
BrowserDownload site_url, mime_type, file_path
    WHERE BrowserDownload mime_type contains "octet-stream"
        AND BrowserDownload file_path contains "foo.exe"
            OR BrowserDownload file_path contains "bar.exe"
            OR BrowserDownload file_path contains "baz.exe"

examples.title: Prevalence - downloads by web referrer and web provider
examples.v2_query:
BrowserDownload referrer, site_url
    WHERE BrowserDownload referrer not equals ""
        AND BrowserDownload site_url not equals ""

examples.title: Specify download by referrer to provider combination
examples.v2_query:
BrowserDownload referrer, site_url
    WHERE BrowserDownload referrer contains "evil_foo.com"
        AND BrowserDownload site_url not equals "drive.google.com"

examples.title: BrowserDownload entries where browser equals "Google Chrome Mozilla Firefox Microsoft Edge"

and.name: BrowserDownload
and.value: Google Chrome Mozilla Firefox Microsoft Edge
